note: Written by hand, in the A1.5 pattern. Every decision carries its warrant and the measurement the warrant rests on. Downstream scripts READ this file; they do not re-derive it.

graph_variant.warrant: A1.5 froze full vs lcc. A3 must not assume; every count depends on it.

warrant: 57 distinct (label, relation) pairs measured from a2_integrity.json.edges_by_label_relation. Not 27 labels, not 36 relations.
A Neo4j relationship has exactly ONE type. label and relation cannot both be types; the node-side 'GEN;Node' multi-label trick has no relationship equivalent.
relation alone collapses INDICATION across DRG-DIS / DRG-PHE / DRG-BPO (57,601 + 1,027 + 62), losing the label distinction that DEFINES the leakage surface (G7).
label alone collapses INDICATION with CONTRAINDICATION inside DRG-DIS.
The (label, relation) PAIR is the unit of meaning: the A7 surface is 7 pairs / 79,949 edges.
57 is far inside Neo4j's usable type range; type-count ergonomics degrade in the thousands.
Double underscore is unambiguously reversible because no label contains '_' and no relation contains '__' (both asserted at emit).
No backticks required: every generated name matches ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ (asserted at emit).

Keeps the anti-join in the language where A2 MEASURED its safety. endpoint_key is orientation-insensitive on the 13 undirected labels, each stored exactly once (G5/M1); a (from,to) join under-removes silently.

The Neo4j store becomes a pure function of sha256-pinned inputs (canon_edges/ + removed_edge_ids.csv), so a3_manifest.json fully describes it. Delete-in-place makes the store 'import, then N mutations' — reproducible only if the mutation script is itself versioned.
Delete-in-place does not avoid the rebuild, it defers it: the deleted edges are gone from the store, so returning to the full graph requires re-emit + re-import anyway.
Cypher therefore never has to EXPRESS the leakage surface. Composite TYPE is retained for query ergonomics and verification, not for deletion correctness.
Neo4j does not reclaim store space on delete without a store copy; a deleted graph stays full-size on disk, so file size cannot serve as a variant tell either.

node_label_scheme.warrant: One uniqueness constraint on (:Node) replaces ten per-label constraints. The importer supports ';'-delimited labels natively.

uniqueness_constraint_key.warrant: A2 froze id as VERBATIM and G2 measured it globally unique across all 190,531 nodes. The how-to's id_curie does not exist as a column; a constraint on a non-existent property is a SILENT NO-OP.

warrant: label + relation: the composite type splits the graph into 57 types; these properties keep cross-group queries (WHERE r.relation='INDICATION') possible without enumerating types.

undirected: REQUIRED. Neo4j always stores relationships directed, and the 13 undirected labels are stored one-way only. Without this property a Cypher author cannot distinguish 'genuinely directed' from 'stored once, semantically symmetric' and will write -[r]-> where -[r]- is required.
endpoint_key: serves A7 verification and per-edge lookup against removed_edge_ids.csv.
Neo4j is a traversal runtime, not a second copy of the data. The canonical Parquet remains authoritative.
properties_json is the only field that could carry embedded newlines and force the slow --multiline-fields=true path across all 21.8M edges.
description is NULL for all 190,531 nodes (errata E4); the demo surface uses name.
Provenance (sources_direct / sources_indirect) stays in Parquet: it is a list, uneven across labels (0% on ANA-GEN and DRG-DRG), and citation-building reads the canonical tables, not the graph.

node_properties_emitted.warrant: id_prefix is carried so demo and eval queries can exclude the 10,997 OBA nodes (biological attributes, not diseases) without a round-trip to Parquet.

node_name_whitespace_normalized.what: CR/LF/TAB inside node.name are replaced with a single space in nodes.csv ONLY.
node_name_whitespace_normalized.warrant: Avoids forcing --multiline-fields=true, which would slow the 21.8M-edge import for a node-only problem. Canonical names in canon_nodes.parquet are untouched. The count of affected rows is measured and recorded in a3_manifest.json; if it is 0 this transformation is a no-op.

variant_marker.warrant: Neo4j Community Edition hosts ONE database and nothing inside it says which variant is loaded. Without a marker you can evaluate against the full graph and never know. a3_verify.py refuses to pass if the marker disagrees with the counts it measures.

query_convention.warrant: 13 of 27 labels are undirected and stored one-way only. Directed patterns silently miss half the graph. Use -[r]-> ONLY where r.undirected = false is checked. Applies to demo subgraph, verification snippets, and every template handed to retrieval.

a7_contract.warrant: a3_emit_csvs.py FATALs without endpoint_key. The (from,to) fallback under-removes on undirected labels with no visible symptom, which is precisely the failure endpoint_key exists to prevent.
